intro: Achnatherum contractum, also known as contracted oat grass, is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine for its purported health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Achnatherum contractum contains bioactive constituents including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and decoctions, often used to support lung health and alleviate symptoms associated with respiratory conditions.
